risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to mixed material use (Al 7075 + CFRP + FR4).
risk.mit: Conduct thermal expansion analysis and adjust fastener preloads accordingly.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and use alignment tools.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity if the frame_shell is not properly reinforced.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis and reinforce the frame_shell as necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference from improperly shielded harnesses.
risk.mit: Use shielded cables and ensure proper routing and grounding.

risk.desc: Risk of payload module misalignment due to snap_fit interface tolerances.
risk.mit: Ensure precise manufacturing and include alignment aids in the design.

risk.desc: Deployment damper may not provide sufficient energy absorption during initial deployment.
risk.mit: Conduct physical testing to validate damper performance and adjust parameters as necessary.

risk.desc: Burn wire release mechanism may fail due to environmental factors.
risk.mit: Include redundant release mechanisms and conduct environmental testing.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may affect hinge functionality over time.
risk.mit: Use materials with low thermal expansion coefficients and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Spring-loaded hinge may not maintain consistent preload over the mission duration.
risk.mit: Regularly monitor hinge preload and adjust as necessary.

risk.desc: Electrical isolation between solar panel and deployment mechanism may degrade over time.
risk.mit: Conduct regular electrical isolation tests and implement maintenance procedures.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently
